seo.title: Enterprise SaaS RAG Pipeline AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for rag pipeline AI agents in enterprise saas. Covers SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR.

content.intro: Deploying RAG pipelines in enterprise SaaS environments introduces compounded risk across data isolation, source fidelity, and contractual compliance — where a single misconfiguration can trigger SLA breaches, GDPR violations, or multi-tenant data leakage. This checklist operationalizes the controls required to safely run document retrieval, vector search, and LLM synthesis at enterprise scale. Each item maps directly to SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR, and CCPA obligations specific to AI-assisted document workflows.

sections.heading: Multi-Tenant Data Isolation & Residency
sections.description: Ensure vector stores, document chunks, and LLM context windows are strictly scoped per tenant, and that data residency obligations from customer contracts are enforced at the infrastructure level.

items.label: Tenant-scoped vector namespace enforcement
items.description: Configure your vector database (e.g., Pinecone namespaces, Weaviate multi-tenancy, pgvector row-level security) so that embedding retrieval queries are hard-filtered by tenant ID before any results are returned — never rely solely on application-layer filtering.

items.label: Data residency routing per customer contract
items.description: Map each enterprise customer's contractual data residency requirement (e.g., EU-only, US-only) to a dedicated deployment region for both the vector store and LLM inference endpoint. Validate routing logic with automated integration tests on every deployment.

items.label: Document ingestion pipeline tenant tagging
items.description: Enforce that every document chunk stored in the vector index carries a non-nullable tenant_id metadata field, validated at ingestion time with a schema enforcement layer that rejects untagged documents before embedding.

items.label: Cross-tenant retrieval penetration testing
items.description: Run quarterly adversarial retrieval tests where a simulated tenant attempts to retrieve documents belonging to another tenant via crafted query embeddings, metadata injection, or prompt manipulation. Document results and remediation actions.

items.label: LLM context window sanitization
items.description: Before assembling the context payload sent to the LLM, programmatically verify that all retrieved document chunks share the same tenant_id as the requesting user session. Reject and alert on any mismatch rather than silently dropping chunks.

items.label: Tenant data deletion propagation
items.description: Implement a verified deletion workflow that removes a tenant's document embeddings from the vector store, source documents from blob storage, and any cached LLM responses within 30 days of a deletion request, with a signed audit record of completion.

items.label: Encryption key isolation per tenant
items.description: Use customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK) or per-tenant key hierarchies for encrypting stored embeddings and source documents, ensuring that key revocation for one tenant does not affect others and satisfies ISO 27001 A.10 controls.

sections.heading: RAG Pipeline Accuracy & Hallucination Controls
sections.description: Implement verification layers across retrieval relevance, context faithfulness, and LLM output grounding to prevent the pipeline from generating plausible but incorrect answers that could drive enterprise business decisions.

items.label: Retrieval relevance scoring threshold
items.description: Set a minimum cosine similarity or reranker confidence threshold (e.g., ≥0.75) below which retrieved chunks are excluded from context assembly. Log and alert when more than 30% of a query's retrieved chunks fall below threshold, indicating a potential out-of-distribution query.

items.label: Source faithfulness evaluation on sampled outputs
items.description: Run an automated faithfulness scorer (e.g., RAGAS faithfulness metric, NLI-based entailment check) on a statistically significant sample of production responses daily. Set an alert threshold if faithfulness scores drop below 0.85 across any tenant cohort.

items.label: Citation accuracy verification
items.description: For every source attribution returned to the user, programmatically verify that the cited document chunk ID exists in the tenant's index, that the chunk text matches the stored version, and that the LLM's claim is entailed by that specific chunk — not just the broader document.

items.label: Hallucination detection guardrail layer
items.description: Deploy a secondary LLM or classifier as a post-generation guardrail that checks whether the synthesized answer introduces claims not present in the assembled context. Flag responses with a hallucination risk score above threshold for human review before delivery.

items.label: Context window overflow handling
items.description: Define an explicit truncation and prioritization strategy (e.g., reranker-based top-k selection) for when retrieved context exceeds the LLM's context window. Never silently truncate — log which chunks were dropped and surface a confidence degradation warning in the response metadata.

items.label: Out-of-scope query detection and refusal
items.description: Implement an intent classifier that detects queries outside the scope of the tenant's indexed document corpus and returns a structured 'insufficient grounding' response rather than allowing the LLM to generate from parametric memory.

items.label: Embedding model version pinning and drift monitoring
items.description: Pin the embedding model version used at ingestion time and enforce the same version at query time. Monitor for semantic drift when upgrading embedding models by running a regression suite on a golden query set before re-indexing production data.

items.label: Adversarial prompt injection detection in queries
items.description: Scan incoming user queries for prompt injection patterns (e.g., instructions to ignore system prompts, role-switching commands) before they are embedded and used for retrieval. Log and block detected injection attempts with a structured audit record.

sections.heading: Audit Logging, Observability & SLA Compliance
sections.description: Maintain immutable, queryable audit trails of every AI-assisted action to satisfy SOC 2 Type II CC7 controls, enterprise contract audit rights, and GDPR Article 30 processing records — while ensuring pipeline latency and availability meet contractual SLAs.

items.label: Immutable audit log for every RAG query lifecycle
items.description: Log each query event with: timestamp, tenant_id, user_id, query text hash, retrieved chunk IDs and scores, context assembly metadata, LLM model version, response hash, and latency. Write logs to an append-only store (e.g., AWS CloudTrail, immutable S3 with Object Lock) that cannot be modified by application-layer credentials.

items.label: AI-assisted action attribution in audit trail
items.description: Tag every downstream action taken based on an AI response (e.g., document summarization exported, answer cited in a ticket) with the originating RAG query ID, enabling full traceability from business action back to source documents for enterprise audit requests.

items.label: SLA latency monitoring with per-tenant alerting
items.description: Instrument p50, p95, and p99 latency for the full RAG pipeline (retrieval + context assembly + LLM inference) per tenant. Configure alerts that fire before SLA breach thresholds are reached, with automated runbooks for common degradation patterns.

items.label: LLM provider outage fallback and SLA impact tracking
items.description: Implement a fallback strategy (e.g., secondary LLM provider, cached response serving, graceful degradation to retrieval-only mode) for LLM API outages. Track and report LLM-provider-caused downtime separately in SLA calculations per enterprise contract terms.

items.label: Audit log retention aligned to contractual requirements
items.description: Retain RAG pipeline audit logs for a minimum of 12 months online and 7 years in cold storage, or per the longest retention period specified across active enterprise customer contracts. Automate retention policy enforcement with lifecycle rules.

items.label: Real-time anomaly detection on retrieval patterns
items.description: Monitor for anomalous retrieval patterns per tenant (e.g., sudden spike in cross-document queries, unusually high chunk retrieval counts) that may indicate data exfiltration attempts or misconfigured integrations. Alert security team within 15 minutes of detection.

items.label: Audit log access controls and segregation of duties
items.description: Restrict audit log read access to a dedicated security/compliance role separate from engineering and operations. Require MFA and log all access to audit logs themselves, satisfying SOC 2 CC6.1 and ISO 27001 A.9.2 access management controls.

sections.heading: Data Privacy, Consent & Regulatory Compliance
sections.description: Operationalize GDPR, CCPA, and enterprise data processing agreement (DPA) requirements across the full RAG pipeline — from document ingestion consent to LLM training data opt-out and subject access request fulfillment.

items.label: Data Processing Agreement coverage for LLM providers
items.description: Ensure a signed DPA is in place with every LLM API provider (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure OpenAI) before routing enterprise customer data through their endpoints. Verify the DPA explicitly covers sub-processor obligations and prohibits training on customer data.

items.label: PII detection and redaction before embedding
items.description: Run a PII detection scan (e.g., using Microsoft Presidio or AWS Comprehend) on all documents before chunking and embedding. Apply configurable redaction or pseudonymization policies per tenant data classification rules, and log redaction events for audit purposes.

items.label: GDPR Article 17 right-to-erasure pipeline
items.description: Implement an automated erasure workflow triggered by data subject deletion requests that: identifies all document chunks containing the subject's data via metadata tagging, deletes embeddings from the vector store, removes source documents, invalidates cached responses, and generates a compliance certificate within 30 days.

items.label: CCPA opt-out propagation to RAG pipeline
items.description: When a California resident exercises their CCPA opt-out of sale/sharing rights, ensure their data is excluded from any RAG pipeline components that involve third-party LLM providers classified as 'service providers' under CCPA. Automate opt-out flag propagation within 24 hours.

items.label: Article 30 Records of Processing Activities for RAG
items.description: Maintain a GDPR Article 30 record that specifically documents the RAG pipeline as a processing activity, including: categories of data processed, LLM sub-processors, data flows across regions, retention periods, and security measures. Review and update quarterly.

items.label: Lawful basis documentation for AI-assisted processing
items.description: Document the lawful basis (e.g., legitimate interest, contractual necessity) for each category of personal data processed through the RAG pipeline. Conduct and document a Legitimate Interest Assessment (LIA) if relying on legitimate interest as the basis.

items.label: Data minimization enforcement in context assembly
items.description: Configure context assembly to retrieve only the minimum number of document chunks necessary to answer the query (enforce a maximum top-k limit per query type). Prohibit retrieval of entire documents when only specific sections are relevant, reducing unnecessary personal data exposure.

items.label: Third-party sub-processor change notification workflow
items.description: Implement a workflow to notify enterprise customers at least 30 days before adding or replacing LLM providers or vector database sub-processors, as required by most enterprise DPAs. Maintain a public sub-processor list updated in real time.

notes.note: RAG pipeline audit logs must satisfy SOC 2 CC7.2 (anomaly detection) and CC6.1 (logical access) controls, as well as ISO 27001 Annex A.12.4 (logging and monitoring). Auditors will specifically examine whether AI-generated outputs are traceable to source documents and whether tenant isolation controls have been independently tested. Ensure your penetration testing scope explicitly includes vector store cross-tenant retrieval attacks.

Article 22 automated decision-making obligations may apply if RAG pipeline outputs directly drive consequential decisions for EU data subjects (e.g., contract recommendations, HR document analysis). Implement human-in-the-loop checkpoints for high-stakes outputs. Article 25 data protection by design requires PII redaction and data minimization to be enforced at the pipeline architecture level, not as optional post-processing steps.

Enterprise SaaS vendors acting as 'service providers' under CCPA must contractually prohibit use of customer personal data for any purpose outside the specified service, including LLM fine-tuning or model improvement. Verify that all LLM API providers have signed service provider agreements with the required CCPA contractual restrictions, and that your own DPA with enterprise customers reflects these downstream obligations.

Enterprise contracts frequently include audit rights clauses requiring you to produce evidence of data isolation and AI processing controls within 30–60 days of a written request. Maintain a pre-packaged compliance evidence bundle (tenant isolation test results, audit log samples, DPA inventory, sub-processor list) that can be delivered within this window. Data residency violations — even transient ones caused by LLM API routing — can constitute material contract breaches triggering termination rights.

cta.heading: Automate Your Enterprise SaaS Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your RAG pipeline against these controls — automating tenant isolation checks, hallucination scoring, audit log validation, and GDPR erasure workflows so your team ships faster without sacrificing enterprise trust.
